Atrial fibrillation.Definition: A disorder characterized by a dysrhythmia without discernible P waves and an irregular ventricular response due to multiple reentry circuits. The rhythm disturbance originates above the ventricles.
Atrial fibrillation.Grade 3: Symptomatic, urgent intervention indicated; device (e.g., pacemaker); ablation; new onset
Atrial fibrillation.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; embolus requiring urgent intervention

Atrial flutter.Definition: A disorder characterized by a dysrhythmia with organized rhythmic atrial contractions with a rate of 200-300 beats per minute. The rhythm disturbance originates in the atria.
Atrial flutter.Grade 3: Symptomatic, urgent intervention indicated; device (e.g., pacemaker); ablation
Atrial flutter.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; embolus requiring urgent intervention

Chest pain - cardiac.Definition: A disorder characterized by substernal discomfort due to insufficient myocardial oxygenation e.g., angina pectoris.
Chest pain - cardiac.Grade 2: Moderate pain; pain on exertion; limiting instrumental ADL; hemodynamically stable
Chest pain - cardiac.Grade 3: Pain at rest; limiting self care ADL; cardiac catheterization; new onset cardiac chest pain; unstable angina

Conduction disorder.Definition: A disorder characterized by pathological irregularities in the cardiac conduction system.

Heart failure.Definition: A disorder characterized by the inability of the heart to pump blood at an adequate volume to meet tissue metabolic requirements, or, the ability to do so only at an elevation in the filling pressure.
Heart failure.Grade 1: Asymptomatic with laboratory (e.g., BNP [B-Natriuretic Peptide ]) or cardiac imaging abnormalities
Heart failure.Grade 2: Symptoms with moderate activity or exertion
Heart failure.Grade 3: Symptoms at rest or with minimal activity or exertion; hospitalization; new onset of symptoms
Heart failure.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; urgent intervention indicated (e.g., continuous IV therapy or mechanical hemodynamic support)

Left ventricular systolic dysfunction.Definition: A disorder characterized by failure of the left ventricle to produce adequate output.
Left ventricular systolic dysfunction.Grade 3: Symptomatic due to drop in ejection fraction responsive to intervention
Left ventricular systolic dysfunction.Grade 4: Refractory or poorly controlled heart failure due to drop in ejection fraction; intervention such as ventricular assist device, intravenous vasopressor support, or heart transplant indicated

Myocarditis.Definition: A disorder characterized by inflammation of the muscle tissue of the heart.
Myocarditis.Grade 2: Symptoms with moderate activity or exertion
Myocarditis.Grade 3: Severe with symptoms at rest or with minimal activity or exertion; intervention indicated; new onset of symptoms
Myocarditis.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; urgent intervention indicated (e.g., continuous IV therapy or mechanical hemodynamic support)

Palpitations.Definition: A disorder characterized by an unpleasant sensation of irregular and/or forceful beating of the heart.

Pericardial effusion.Definition: A disorder characterized by fluid collection within the pericardial sac, usually due to inflammation.
Pericardial effusion.Grade 2: Asymptomatic effusion size small to moderate

Pericardial tamponade.Definition: A disorder characterized by an increase in intrapericardial pressure due to the collection of blood or fluid in the pericardium.

Pericarditis.Definition: A disorder characterized by irritation to the layers of the pericardium (the protective sac around the heart).
Pericarditis.Grade 1: Asymptomatic, ECG or physical findings (e.g., rub) consistent with pericarditis
Pericarditis.Grade 3: Pericarditis with physiologic consequences (e.g., pericardial constriction)

Restrictive cardiomyopathy.Definition: A disorder characterized by an inability of the ventricles to fill with blood because the myocardium (heart muscle) stiffens and loses its flexibility.
Restrictive cardiomyopathy.Grade 2: Symptomatic without signs of heart failure
Restrictive cardiomyopathy.Grade 3: Symptomatic heart failure or other cardiac symptoms, responsive to intervention; new onset of symptoms
Restrictive cardiomyopathy.Grade 4: Refractory heart failure or other poorly controlled cardiac symptoms

Right ventricular dysfunction.Definition: A disorder characterized by impairment of right ventricular function associated with low ejection fraction and a decrease in motility of the right ventricular wall.
Right ventricular dysfunction.Grade 1: Asymptomatic with laboratory (e.g., BNP [B-Natriuretic Peptide ]) or cardiac imaging abnormalities
Right ventricular dysfunction.Grade 2: Symptoms with moderate activity or exertion
Right ventricular dysfunction.Grade 3: Severe symptoms, associated with hypoxia, right heart failure; oxygen indicated
Right ventricular dysfunction.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; urgent intervention indicated (e.g., ventricular assist device); heart transplant indicated

Ventricular fibrillation.Definition: A disorder characterized by a dysrhythmia without discernible QRS complexes due to rapid repetitive excitation of myocardial fibers without coordinated contraction of the ventricles.

Ventricular tachycardia.Definition: A disorder characterized by a dysrhythmia with a heart rate greater than 100 beats per minute that originates distal to the bundle of His.

Cardiac troponin I increased.Definition: A finding based on laboratory test results that indicate increased levels of cardiac troponin I in a biological specimen.
Cardiac troponin I increased.Grade 1: Levels above the upper limit of normal and below the level of myocardial infarction as defined by the manufacturer
Cardiac troponin I increased.Grade 3: Levels consistent with myocardial infarction as defined by the manufacturer

Cardiac troponin T increased.Definition: A finding based on laboratory test results that indicate increased levels of cardiac troponin T in a biological specimen.
Cardiac troponin T increased.Grade 1: Levels above the upper limit of normal and below the level of myocardial infarction as defined by the manufacturer
Cardiac troponin T increased.Grade 3: Levels consistent with myocardial infarction as defined by the manufacturer

Electrocardiogram QT corrected interval.Definition: A finding of a cardiac dysrhythmia characterized by an abnormally long corrected QT interval.
Electrocardiogram QT corrected interval.Grade 3: Average QTc >= 501 ms; >60 ms change from baseline
Electrocardiogram QT corrected interval.Grade 4: Torsade de pointes; polymorphic ventricular tachycardia; signs/symptoms of serious arrhythmia

Electrocardiogram T wave abnormal.Definition: A disorder characterized by Electrocardiogram T wave amplitude changes.

Hyperthyroidism.Definition: A disorder characterized by excessive levels of thyroid hormone in the body. Common causes include an overactive thyroid gland or thyroid hormone overdose.
Hyperthyroidism.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Hyperthyroidism.Grade 2: Symptomatic; thyroid suppression therapy indicated; limiting instrumental ADL
Hyperthyroidism.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; limiting self care ADL; hospitalization indicated

Hypothyroidism.Definition: A disorder characterized by a decrease in production of thyroid hormone by the thyroid gland.
Hypothyroidism.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Hypothyroidism.Grade 2: Symptomatic; thyroid replacement indicated; limiting instrumental ADL
Hypothyroidism.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; limiting self care ADL; hospitalization indicated

Thyroid stimulating hormone increased.Definition: A disorder characterized by an increase in thyroid stimulating hormone.
Thyroid stimulating hormone increased.Grade 1: TSH increased and no intervention initiated

Abdominal pain.Definition: A disorder characterized by a sensation of marked discomfort in the abdominal region.
Abdominal pain.Grade 3: Severe pain; limiting self care ADL

Bloating.Definition: A disorder characterized by subject-reported feeling of uncomfortable fullness of the abdomen.
Bloating.Grade 1: No change in bowel function or oral intake
Bloating.Grade 2: Symptomatic, decreased oral intake; change in bowel function

Colitis.Definition: A disorder characterized by inflammation of the colon.
Colitis.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Colitis.Grade 2: Abdominal pain; mucus or blood in stool

Colonic hemorrhage.Definition: A disorder characterized by bleeding from the colon.
Colonic hemorrhage.Grade 3: Transfusion indicated; invasive intervention indicated; hospitalization

Colonic perforation.Definition: A disorder characterized by a rupture in the colonic wall.

Colonic ulcer.Definition: A disorder characterized by a circumscribed, erosive lesion on the mucosal surface of the colon.
Colonic ulcer.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Colonic ulcer.Grade 3: Severely altered GI function; TPN indicated; elective invasive intervention indicated
Colonic ulcer.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; urgent operative intervention indicated

Diarrhea.Definition: A disorder characterized by an increase in frequency and/or loose or watery bowel movements.
Diarrhea.Grade 1: Increase of <4 stools per day over baseline; mild increase in ostomy output compared to baseline
Diarrhea.Grade 2: Increase of 4 - 6 stools per day over baseline; moderate increase in ostomy output compared to baseline; limiting instrumental ADL
Diarrhea.Grade 3: Increase of >=7 stools per day over baseline; hospitalization indicated; severe increase in ostomy output compared to baseline; limiting self care ADL

Enterocolitis.Definition: A disorder characterized by inflammation of the small and large intestines.
Enterocolitis.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Enterocolitis.Grade 2: Abdominal pain; mucus or blood in stool
Enterocolitis.Grade 3: Severe or persistent abdominal pain; fever; ileus; peritoneal signs

Fecal incontinence.Definition: A disorder characterized by inability to control the escape of stool from the rectum.
Fecal incontinence.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; elective operative intervention indicated

Proctitis.Definition: A disorder characterized by inflammation of the rectum.
Proctitis.Grade 2: Symptomatic (e.g., rectal discomfort, passing blood or mucus); medical intervention indicated; limiting instrumental ADL
Proctitis.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; fecal urgency or stool incontinence; limiting self care ADL

Rectal mucositis.Definition: A disorder characterized by ulceration or inflammation of the mucous membrane of the rectum.
Rectal mucositis.Grade 1: Asymptomatic or mild symptoms; intervention not indicated
Rectal mucositis.Grade 2: Symptomatic; medical intervention indicated; limiting instrumental ADL
Rectal mucositis.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; limiting self care ADL
Rectal mucositis.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; urgent operative intervention indicated

Typhlitis.Definition: A disorder characterized by necrotizing enterocolitis in neutropenic patients.
Typhlitis.Grade 3: Symptomatic (e.g., abdominal pain, fever, change in bowel habits with ileus); peritoneal signs
Typhlitis.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; urgent operative intervention indicated

Budd-Chiari syndrome.Definition: A disorder characterized by occlusion of the hepatic veins and typically presents with abdominal pain, ascites and hepatomegaly.
Budd-Chiari syndrome.Grade 3: Severe or medically significant but not immediately life-threatening; hospitalization or prolongation of existing hospitalization indicated; asterixis; mild encephalopathy
Budd-Chiari syndrome.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; moderate to severe encephalopathy; coma

Hepatic failure.Definition: A disorder characterized by the inability of the liver to metabolize chemicals in the body. Laboratory test results reveal abnormal plasma levels of ammonia, bilirubin, lactic dehydrogenase, alkaline phosphatase, aminotransferase, and/or prolongation of prothrombin time (INR.) Drug-induced liever injury (DILI) as defined by Hy's Law.
Hepatic failure.Grade 3: Asterixis; mild encephalopathy; drug-induced liver injury (DILI); limiting self care ADL
Hepatic failure.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; moderate to severe encephalopathy; coma

Hepatic hemorrhage.Definition: A disorder characterized by bleeding from the liver.
Hepatic hemorrhage.Grade 3: Transfusion indicated; invasive intervention indicated; hospitalization

Hepatic necrosis.Definition: A disorder characterized by a necrotic process occurring in the hepatic parenchyma.
Hepatic necrosis.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; urgent invasive intervention indicated

Hepatic pain.Definition: A disorder characterized by a sensation of marked discomfort in the liver region.
Hepatic pain.Grade 3: Severe pain; limiting self care ADL

Sinusoidal obstruction syndrome.Definition: A disorder characterized by severe hepatic injury as a result of the blood vessels of the liver becoming inflamed and/or blocked.
Sinusoidal obstruction syndrome.Grade 2: Blood bilirubin 2-5 mg/dL; minor interventions required (i.e., blood product, diuretic, oxygen)
Sinusoidal obstruction syndrome.Grade 3: Blood bilirubin >5 mg/dL; coagulation modifier indicated (e.g., defibrotide); reversal of flow on ultrasound
Sinusoidal obstruction syndrome.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences (e.g., ventilatory support, dialysis, plasmapheresis, peritoneal drainage)

Alanine aminotransferase increased.Definition: A finding based on laboratory test results that indicate an increase in the level of alanine aminotransferase (ALT or SGPT) in the blood specimen.
Alanine aminotransferase increased.Grade 1: >ULN - 3.0 x ULN if baseline was normal; 1.5 - 3.0 x baseline if baseline was abnormal
Alanine aminotransferase increased.Grade 2: >3.0 - 5.0 x ULN if baseline was normal; >3.0 - 5.0 x baseline if baseline was abnormal
Alanine aminotransferase increased.Grade 3: >5.0 - 20.0 x ULN if baseline was normal; >5.0 - 20.0 x baseline if baseline was abnormal
Alanine aminotransferase increased.Grade 4: >20.0 x ULN if baseline was normal; >20.0 x baseline if baseline was abnormal

Alkaline phosphatase increased.Definition: A finding based on laboratory test results that indicate an increase in the level of alkaline phosphatase in a blood specimen.
Alkaline phosphatase increased.Grade 1: >ULN - 2.5 x ULN if baseline was normal; 2.0 - 2.5 x baseline if baseline was abnormal
Alkaline phosphatase increased.Grade 2: >2.5 - 5.0 x ULN if baseline was normal; >2.5 - 5.0 x baseline if baseline was abnormal
Alkaline phosphatase increased.Grade 3: >5.0 - 20.0 x ULN if baseline was normal; >5.0 - 20.0 x baseline if baseline was abnormal
Alkaline phosphatase increased.Grade 4: >20.0 x ULN if baseline was normal; >20.0 x baseline if baseline was abnormal

Aspartate aminotransferase increased.Definition: A finding based on laboratory test results that indicate an increase in the level of aspartate aminotransferase (AST or SGOT) in a blood specimen.
Aspartate aminotransferase increased.Grade 1: >ULN - 3.0 x ULN if baseline was normal; 1.5 - 3.0 x baseline if baseline was abnormal
Aspartate aminotransferase increased.Grade 2: >3.0 - 5.0 x ULN if baseline was normal; >3.0 - 5.0 x baseline if baseline was abnormal
Aspartate aminotransferase increased.Grade 3: >5.0 - 20.0 x ULN if baseline was normal; >5.0 - 20.0 x baseline if baseline was abnormal
Aspartate aminotransferase increased.Grade 4: >20.0 x ULN if baseline was normal; >20.0 x baseline if baseline was abnormal

Blood bilirubin increased.Definition: A finding based on laboratory test results that indicate an abnormally high level of bilirubin in the blood. Excess bilirubin is associated with jaundice.
Blood bilirubin increased.Grade 1: >ULN - 1.5 x ULN if baseline was normal; > 1.0 - 1.5 x baseline if baseline was abnormal
Blood bilirubin increased.Grade 2: >1.5 - 3.0 x ULN if baseline was normal; >1.5 - 3.0 x baseline if baseline was abnormal
Blood bilirubin increased.Grade 3: >3.0 - 10.0 x ULN if baseline was normal; >3.0 - 10.0 x baseline if baseline was abnormal
Blood bilirubin increased.Grade 4: >10.0 x ULN if baseline was normal; >10.0 x baseline if baseline was abnormal

Adult respiratory distress syndrome.Definition: A disorder characterized by progressive and life-threatening pulmonary distress in the absence of an underlying pulmonary condition, usually following major trauma or surgery.
Adult respiratory distress syndrome.Grade 3: Present with radiologic findings; intubation not indicated
Adult respiratory distress syndrome.Grade 4: Life-threatening respiratory or hemodynamic compromise; intubation or urgent intervention indicated

Aspiration.Definition: A disorder characterized by inhalation of solids or liquids into the lungs.
Aspiration.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Aspiration.Grade 2: Altered eating habits; coughing or choking episodes after eating or swallowing; medical intervention indicated (e.g., suction or oxygen)
Aspiration.Grade 3: Dyspnea and pneumonia symptoms (e.g., aspiration pneumonia); hospitalization indicated; unable to aliment orally
Aspiration.Grade 4: Life-threatening respiratory or hemodynamic compromise; intubation or urgent intervention indicated

Atelectasis.Definition: A disorder characterized by the collapse of part or the entire lung.
Atelectasis.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Atelectasis.Grade 2: Symptomatic (e.g., dyspnea, cough); medical intervention indicated (e.g., chest physiotherapy, suctioning); bronchoscopic suctioning
Atelectasis.Grade 3: Supplemental oxygen indicated; hospitalization or elective operative intervention indicated (e.g., stent, laser)
Atelectasis.Grade 4: Life-threatening respiratory or hemodynamic compromise; intubation or urgent intervention indicated

Bronchopulmonary hemorrhage.Definition: A disorder characterized by bleeding from the bronchial wall and/or lung parenchyma.
Bronchopulmonary hemorrhage.Grade 2: Moderate symptoms; invasive intervention not indicated
Bronchopulmonary hemorrhage.Grade 3: Transfusion indicated; invasive intervention indicated; hospitalization
Bronchopulmonary hemorrhage.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; intubation or urgent intervention indicated

Cough.Definition: A disorder characterized by sudden, often repetitive, spasmodic contraction of the thoracic cavity, resulting in violent release of air from the lungs and usually accompanied by a distinctive sound.
Cough.Grade 2: Moderate symptoms, medical intervention indicated; limiting instrumental ADL
Cough.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; limiting self care ADL

Dyspnea.Definition: A disorder characterized by an uncomfortable sensation of difficulty breathing.
Dyspnea.Grade 1: Shortness of breath with moderate exertion
Dyspnea.Grade 2: Shortness of breath with minimal exertion; limiting instrumental ADL
Dyspnea.Grade 3: Shortness of breath at rest; limiting self care ADL

Hypoxia.Definition: A disorder characterized by a decrease in the level of oxygen in the body.
Hypoxia.Grade 2: Decreased oxygen saturation with exercise (e.g., pulse oximeter <88%); intermittent supplemental oxygen
Hypoxia.Grade 3: Decreased oxygen saturation at rest (e.g., pulse oximeter <88% or PaO2 <=55 mm Hg)
Hypoxia.Grade 4: Life-threatening airway compromise; urgent intervention indicated (e.g., tracheotomy or intubation)

Pleural effusion.Definition: A disorder characterized by an increase in amounts of fluid within the pleural cavity. Symptoms include shortness of breath, cough and marked chest discomfort.
Pleural effusion.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Pleural effusion.Grade 2: Symptomatic; intervention indicated (e.g., diuretics or therapeutic thoracentesis)
Pleural effusion.Grade 3: Symptomatic with respiratory distress and hypoxia; operative intervention including chest tube or pleurodesis indicated
Pleural effusion.Grade 4: Life-threatening respiratory or hemodynamic compromise; intubation or urgent intervention indicated

Pleuritic pain.Definition: A disorder characterized by a sensation of marked discomfort in the pleura.
Pleuritic pain.Grade 3: Severe pain; limiting self care ADL

Pneumonitis.Definition: A disorder characterized by inflammation focally or diffusely affecting the lung parenchyma.
Pneumonitis.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Pneumonitis.Grade 2: Symptomatic; medical intervention indicated; limiting instrumental ADL
Pneumonitis.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; limiting self care ADL; oxygen indicated
Pneumonitis.Grade 4: Life-threatening respiratory compromise; urgent intervention indicated (e.g., tracheotomy or intubation)

Pulmonary edema.Definition: A disorder characterized by accumulation of fluid in the lung tissues that causes a disturbance of the gas exchange that may lead to respiratory failure.
Pulmonary edema.Grade 1: Radiologic findings only; minimal dyspnea on exertion
Pulmonary edema.Grade 2: Moderate dyspnea on exertion; medical intervention indicated; limiting instrumental ADL
Pulmonary edema.Grade 3: Severe dyspnea or dyspnea at rest; oxygen indicated; limiting self care ADL
Pulmonary edema.Grade 4: Life-threatening respiratory compromise; urgent intervention or intubation with ventilatory support indicated

Pulmonary fibrosis.Definition: A disorder characterized by the replacement of the lung tissue by connective tissue, leading to progressive dyspnea, respiratory failure or right heart failure.
Pulmonary fibrosis.Grade 1: Radiologic pulmonary fibrosis <25% of lung volume associated with hypoxia
Pulmonary fibrosis.Grade 2: Evidence of pulmonary hypertension; radiographic pulmonary fibrosis 25 - 50% associated with hypoxia
Pulmonary fibrosis.Grade 3: Severe hypoxia; evidence of right-sided heart failure; radiographic pulmonary fibrosis >50 - 75%
Pulmonary fibrosis.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences (e.g., hemodynamic/pulmonary complications); intubation with ventilatory support indicated; radiographic pulmonary fibrosis >75% with severe honeycombing

Respiratory failure.Definition: A disorder characterized by impaired gas exchange by the respiratory system resulting in hypoxia and a decrease in oxygenation of the tissues that may be associated with an increase in arterial levels of carbon dioxide.
Respiratory failure.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; urgent intervention, intubation, or ventilatory support indicated

Bullous dermatitis.Definition: A disorder characterized by inflammation of the skin characterized by the presence of bullae which are filled with fluid.
Bullous dermatitis.Grade 2: Blisters covering 10 - 30% BSA; painful blisters; limiting instrumental ADL
Bullous dermatitis.Grade 3: Blisters covering >30% BSA; limiting self care ADL
Bullous dermatitis.Grade 4: Blisters covering >30% BSA; associated with fluid or electrolyte abnormalities; ICU care or burn unit indicated

Dry skin.Definition: A disorder characterized by flaky and dull skin; the pores are generally fine, the texture is a papery thin texture.
Dry skin.Grade 1: Covering <10% BSA and no associated erythema or pruritus
Dry skin.Grade 2: Covering 10 - 30% BSA and associated with erythema or pruritus; limiting instrumental ADL
Dry skin.Grade 3: Covering >30% BSA and associated with pruritus; limiting self care ADL

Eczema.Definition: A disorder characterized by skin which becomes itchy, red, inflamed, crusty, thick, scaly, and/or forms blisters.
Eczema.Grade 1: Asymptomatic or mild symptoms; additional medical intervention over baseline not indicated
Eczema.Grade 2: Moderate; topical or oral intervention indicated; additional medical intervention over baseline indicated
Eczema.Grade 3: Severe or medically significant but not immediately life-threatening; IV intervention indicated

Erythema multiforme.Definition: A disorder characterized by target lesions (a pink-red ring around a pale center).
Erythema multiforme.Grade 1: Target lesions covering <10% BSA and not associated with skin tenderness
Erythema multiforme.Grade 2: Target lesions covering 10 - 30% BSA and associated with skin tenderness
Erythema multiforme.Grade 3: Target lesions covering >30% BSA and associated with oral or genital erosions
Erythema multiforme.Grade 4: Target lesions covering >30% BSA; associated with fluid or electrolyte abnormalities; ICU care or burn unit indicated

Erythroderma.Definition: A disorder characterized by generalized inflammatory erythema and exfoliation. The inflammatory process involves > 90% of the body surface area.
Erythroderma.Grade 2: Erythema covering >90% BSA without associated symptoms; limiting instrumental ADL
Erythroderma.Grade 3: Erythema covering >90% BSA with associated symptoms (e.g., pruritus or tenderness); limiting self care ADL
Erythroderma.Grade 4: Erythema covering >90% BSA with associated fluid or electrolyte abnormalities; ICU care or burn unit indicated

Pain of skin.Definition: A disorder characterized by a sensation of marked discomfort in the skin.
Pain of skin.Grade 3: Severe pain; limiting self care ADL

Pruritus.Definition: A disorder characterized by an intense itching sensation.
Pruritus.Grade 1: Mild or localized; topical intervention indicated
Pruritus.Grade 2: Widespread and intermittent; skin changes from scratching (e.g., edema, papulation, excoriations, lichenification, oozing/crusts); oral intervention indicated; limiting instrumental ADL
Pruritus.Grade 3: Widespread and constant; limiting self care ADL or sleep; systemic corticosteroid or immunosuppressive therapy indicated

Rash acneiform.Definition: A disorder characterized by an eruption of papules and pustules, typically appearing in face, scalp, upper chest and back.
Rash acneiform.Grade 1: Papules and/or pustules covering <10% BSA, which may or may not be associated with symptoms of pruritus or tenderness
Rash acneiform.Grade 2: Papules and/or pustules covering 10 - 30% BSA, which may or may not be associated with symptoms of pruritus or tenderness; associated with psychosocial impact; limiting instrumental ADL; papules and/or pustules covering > 30% BSA with or without mild symptoms
Rash acneiform.Grade 3: Papules and/or pustules covering >30% BSA with moderate or severe symptoms; limiting self-care ADL; associated with local superinfection with oral antibiotics indicated
Rash acneiform.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; papules and/or pustules covering any % BSA, which may or may not be associated with symptoms of pruritus or tenderness and are associated with extensive superinfection with IV antibiotics indicated

Rash maculo-papular.Definition: A disorder characterized by the presence of macules (flat) and papules (elevated). Also known as morbillform rash, it is one of the most common cutaneous adverse events, frequently affecting the upper trunk, spreading centripetally and associated with pruritis.
Rash maculo-papular.Grade 1: Macules/papules covering <10% BSA with or without symptoms (e.g., pruritus, burning, tightness)
Rash maculo-papular.Grade 2: Macules/papules covering 10 - 30% BSA with or without symptoms (e.g., pruritus, burning, tightness); limiting instrumental ADL; rash covering > 30% BSA with or without mild symptoms
Rash maculo-papular.Grade 3: Macules/papules covering >30% BSA with moderate or severe symptoms; limiting self care ADL

Skin ulceration.Definition: A disorder characterized by a circumscribed, erosive lesion on the skin.
Skin ulceration.Grade 1: Combined area of ulcers <1 cm; nonblanchable erythema of intact skin with associated warmth or edema
Skin ulceration.Grade 2: Combined area of ulcers 1 - 2 cm; partial thickness skin loss involving skin or subcutaneous fat
Skin ulceration.Grade 3: Combined area of ulcers >2 cm; full-thickness skin loss involving damage to or necrosis of subcutaneous tissue that may extend down to fascia
Skin ulceration.Grade 4: Any size ulcer with extensive destruction, tissue necrosis, or damage to muscle, bone, or supporting structures with or without full thickness skin loss

Stevens-Johnson syndrome.Definition: A disorder characterized by less than 10% total body skin area separation of dermis. The syndrome is thought to be a hypersensitivity complex affecting the skin and the mucous membranes.
Stevens-Johnson syndrome.Grade 3: Skin sloughing covering <10% BSA with associated signs (e.g., erythema, purpura, epidermal detachment, and mucous membrane detachment)
Stevens-Johnson syndrome.Grade 4: Skin sloughing covering 10 - 30% BSA with associated signs (e.g., erythema, purpura, epidermal detachment and mucous membrane detachment)

Toxic epidermal necrolysis.Definition: A disorder characterized by greater than 30% total body skin area separation of dermis. The syndrome is thought to be a hypersensitivity complex affecting the skin and the mucous membranes.
Toxic epidermal necrolysis.Grade 4: Skin sloughing covering >=30% BSA with associated symptoms (e.g., erythema, purpura, or epidermal detachment)

Urticaria.Definition: A disorder characterized by an itchy skin eruption characterized by wheals with pale interiors and well-defined red margins.
Urticaria.Grade 1: Urticarial lesions covering <10% BSA; topical intervention indicated
Urticaria.Grade 2: Urticarial lesions covering 10 - 30% BSA; oral intervention indicated
Urticaria.Grade 3: Urticarial lesions covering >30% BSA; IV intervention indicated
